content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento. CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fa:-< (916) 322-0886 
Kenneth Moser 
11280 Spica Drive 
San Diego, CA 92126 
Re: Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. W-12-033 
Dear Mr. Moser: 
February 15, 2012 
This letter is in response to your request for written advice of February 13, 2012. Your 
inquiry primarily presents issues which are outside the purview of the Political Reform Act 
("Act") . The Commission does not advise on the Public Records Act. You may want to contact 
private counsel or the Attorney General's Office for advice. 
Should you have any additional questions regarding this matter, please contact the 
Commission at (916) 322-5660. 
Sincerely, 
0--,  
Zackery P. Morazzini 
General Counsel 
ZPM:jww 
1 Government Code sections 81000-91014. Commis:ion regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109; 
18996, of the California code of Regulations.

sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asked for FPPC advice on matters related to the Public Records Act, which falls outside the Political Reform Act's scope.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide advice because the inquiry presents issues outside the purview of the Political Reform Act, and recommends the requestor contact private counsel or the Attorney General's Office instead.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter, not a standard advice letter with question and conclusion sections. The document lacks verbatim Q/C sections but clearly indicates the FPPC is declining jurisdiction. File number format is W-12-033 (likely indicating a withdrawal or declination case).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requestor asked for FPPC advice on matters related to the Public Records Act, which falls outside the Political Reform Act's scope.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC declines to provide advice because the inquiry presents issues outside the purview of the Political Reform Act, and recommends the requestor contact private counsel or the Attorney General's Office instead.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines to provide written advice to Kenneth Moser on his February 13, 2012 inquiry, determining that his questions regarding the Public Records Act fall outside the jurisdiction of the Political Reform Act.
